fields.description:
## Why

With the gated server surface in place (this epic's server-scaffolding story, on `depends_on`), the
per-ticket audit page must let a human answer, fast: "did this ticket pass its gates, and if not why —
and can I trust how the machine decided that?" Primary users are BOTH a spot-checking auditor (verdict
+ real problems at a glance) and a gate-debugger (full per-finding provenance). Design goal:
verdict-first, findings-second, provenance-on-demand. This design is the product of two rounds of HCD
critique (finding-centric IA + a neutral threshold meter).

## Dependencies (all consumed from the LANDED read layer — this story modifies NO data model)

This story is a pure function of the audit read layer already merged on `main`
(`rebar.audit.read.audit_trail(ticket_id) -> dict`). It consumes ONLY what that contract exposes and
does **not** modify any sidecar, read helper, or schema (data-model changes are epic-1's scope, OUT of
scope here). Fields available per the landed contract:

- `trail["ticket"]` = `rebar.show_ticket(...)` — title, status, assignee, description, acceptance
  criteria, and the ticket-level `attestations` / `signature` (used for the single ticket-level
  certified indicator below).
- `trail["plan_reviews"]` = newest-first list of `plan_review_result_v2` payloads. Each carries:
  `verdict`, `model`, `impact_model_version` (the gate/model version shown as a trust caption),
  `material_fingerprint`, `coverage`, `coaching[]` (`{move_id, finding_refs, move_name, subject,
  coaching}`), and `findings[]` (the FULL pooled set). Each finding carries `finding`, `criteria`,
  `location`, `evidence`, `scenarios`, `impact`, `suggested_fix`, `verification`, `validity`,
  `priority`, `decision` (`block|advisory|overflow|indeterminate|dropped`... see series below),
  `reason`, `block_threshold`, `blocking_enabled`, `norm_id`. **No per-round timestamp and no per-round
  signature are exposed** by `all_review_results` (it returns only the inner payload) — so the page
  MUST NOT claim a per-round timestamp or a per-round "certified" badge.
- `trail["code_reviews"]` = list of `{ticket_id, sidecars[]}`; each sidecar is `code_review_result_v2`
  with SEPARATE buckets `blocking/advisory/dropped/indeterminate` + `coaching`.
- `trail["completion"]` = `{attestation, sidecar} | None`. The `sidecar` is EITHER a
  `completion_verifier_pass_v1` record carrying `criteria[]` (`{criterion, met, citation, kind}`, `kind
  ∈ {codebase-verifiable, operator-attested}`) OR a `completion_verifier_fail_v1` verdict that carries
  **failures-only `findings[]` and NO `criteria[]`**. The completion panel MUST handle both.

### The decision series (convergence viz + finding grouping)

Plan-review pools findings into up to five `decision` buckets: `blocking`, `advisory`, `overflow`,
`indeterminate`, `dropped`. For BOTH the finding grouping and the convergence visualization the page
renders **four canonical series**, merging `overflow` into `advisory` (both are non-blocking surfaced
findings): **BLOCKING · ADVISORY (incl. overflow) · DROPPED · INDETERMINATE**. Code-review's native
buckets map to the same four series (its `advisory` already includes overflow-equivalents).

## What

A read-only, single-column, finding-centric page at `/ticket/<id>`, built from native
`<details>/<summary>` (no JS — keyboard + screen-reader support come free), one consistent chevron
affordance, and a real `h2`/`h3` heading outline.

- **Ticket header (compact, collapsible):** title, status, assignee; description + full acceptance
  criteria behind a "details" toggle (not a persistent side rail). A **single ticket-level certified
  indicator** (a quiet badge near the header) is shown when `trail["ticket"]` has a valid plan-review
  attestation — sourced from the ticket's `attestations`/`signature`, shown ONCE, never per-round.
- **Gate-status strip (sticky):** all three gate verdicts shown AT ONCE, each with a magnitude count
  (`PLAN ✗ FAIL · 3 blocking` / `COMPLETION ✓ PASS · 8/8 met` / `CODE ⦸ not run`). Each chip is an
  in-page anchor that JUMPS to that gate's detail section. Answers pass/fail (and roughly how bad) in
  zero clicks.
- **Detail sections in FIXED canonical order** (plan → completion → code) — the order never changes as
  verdicts change (preserves spatial memory); severity is carried by the strip + a colored heading
  treatment on a failing section, NOT by reordering.
- **Plan-review and code-review sections** (each with round history):
  - Round navigation via a server-rendered `◂ round n of N ▸` selector. No JS: the selector items are
    anchor links to the SAME page with a query string `?<gate>_round=<n>` (e.g. `?plan_round=2`); the
    server reads that param and renders the selected round's content (default = latest / newest). An
    out-of-range or absent param falls back to the latest round.
  - Per-round **convergence visualization** adapts to round count with a STABLE per-form DOM marker:
    **≤3 rounds → `<div class="conv-bars">`** with one `<div class="conv-bar">` per round (small
    multiples); **≥4 rounds → `<svg class="conv-line">`** with one `<polyline>` per **non-empty
    decision series** (of the four canonical series above — so at most 4 polylines, one per series that
    has data across the rounds, NOT one per round). Either form is accompanied by a visually-hidden
    `<table>` giving the per-round per-series counts for screen readers (WCAG 1.1.1).
  - A round headline verdict, and findings **grouped by decision series**: BLOCKING (expanded, fully
    legible) → ADVISORY / DROPPED / INDETERMINATE (each a quiet one-line collapsed group with a count),
    sorted by `priority` within a group. No "collapse all" control (that needs JS); each group is an
    independent native `<details>` (blocking open, the rest closed).
  - **The finding is the atomic unit.** A finding row (chevron = expandable) opens IN PLACE to the
    four-pass provenance read as ONE narrative under human labels — Finding (pass-1 `finding`, mapped
    `criteria`, `location`, `evidence`, `scenarios`, `impact`, `suggested_fix`) → Verification (pass-2
    `verification` sub-answers + severity attributes) → Decision (pass-3 `decision` + `reason`) →
    Coaching (pass-4 matched `coaching` move + note) — contained in a subtle indented block that reads
    as one unit. `norm_id` is a demoted small metadata line.
  - **Threshold meter = a neutral per-finding meter:** a 0–1 bar filled to `priority` with a tick mark
    at the finding's OWN resolved `block_threshold` (the per-finding value pass-3 used, persisted on the
    finding — the server reads it straight off the record, doing NO runtime `criteria_routing` lookup).
    Whether the fill crosses the tick IS the decision, read spatially. The fill color inherits the
    decision-series color (blocking = alert hue; advisory/dropped/indeterminate = neutral). **v1
    fallback:** a legacy `plan_review_result_v1` finding lacks `block_threshold`/`priority`; when either
    is absent the meter is omitted and replaced by a quiet "threshold not recorded (v1)" caption, so the
    row still renders.
- **Completion panel** (criterion-centric, single-pass, NO chevron — the honest grammar difference from
  finding rows):
  - **PASS sidecar (`completion_verifier_pass_v1`, has `criteria[]`):** one row per criterion from
    `criteria[]` — met glyph + `kind` badge (`codebase-verifiable` / `operator-attested`) + `citation`.
    An `operator-attested` criterion lacking a recorded attestation is explicitly flagged.
  - **FAIL sidecar (`completion_verifier_fail_v1`, NO `criteria[]`) or absent `criteria[]`:** a fallback
    path renders the failure `findings[]` (one row per unmet criterion the verdict cites) under an
    explicit "verdict FAIL — criteria unmet" heading, so the panel never silently breaks.
- **Empty/partial states:** each absent gate shows a quiet, text-only "not yet run" panel with a real
  `h2`/`h3` heading — never hidden.
- **Trust signals** in each round header (a quiet caption): the gate/model version (`model` +
  `impact_model_version`) and the round `verdict`. (NO per-round timestamp and NO per-round certified
  badge — neither has a landed per-round source; the single ticket-level certified indicator lives in
  the header instead.)
- **Visual system:** a strict 3-level type scale; saturated color reserved for verdict + decision-series
  chips only (everything else neutral grey); severity is always encoded by glyph + label + color (never
  color-only); scores shown to one decimal.

## Approach rationale

Finding-centric IA (finding = row; four passes = its in-place provenance) was chosen over
pass-as-navigation after HCD critique: passes are a pipeline artifact, but auditors reason in findings;
co-locating a finding's story answers "why this finding" in one expand and serves both audiences without
a second view. The neutral threshold meter replaces a signed green/red delta whose green=cleared-bar
reading collided with the traffic-light convention (green blocking findings on a failed review read as
"healthy"); inheriting the decision-series color makes strength and severity one coherent signal. The
tick is the finding's OWN resolved threshold (the per-finding value pass-3 used, read straight off the
record — no re-derivation, no round-level scalar, which does not exist). Everything is server-rendered
from the read layer over native no-JS `<details>` + jump-linked anchors + query-param round selection;
single-column stacking survives narrow width at 200% zoom (WCAG 1.4.10).

## Scope

- `src/rebar/audit/server.py` — add a `/ticket/<id>` route consuming `rebar.audit.read.audit_trail`,
  plus a tiny per-gate round selector reading `?<gate>_round=<n>` and the four-series mapping helper.
- New `src/rebar/audit/templates/*.html` (Jinja2) — the page + sticky gate-strip + gate-section +
  finding-provenance + completion-panel + empty-state partials; inline CSS; the convergence
  visualization + threshold meter as inline SVG/CSS (no external JS/asset); each collapsible is a native
  `<details>`. The threshold meter reads each finding's persisted `block_threshold`/`priority`/`decision`
  (with the v1 fallback); the completion panel reads the read layer's completion record (PASS `criteria[]`
  or the FAIL fallback). Each file within module-size policy (< 500 LOC; split partials along real seams,
  none < 100 LOC).

## Compatibility & rollback

Additive on top of the server-scaffolding story's gated surface; still default-off, loopback-only,
read-only. Rollback = the epic's flag / extra. No data or schema change (this story reads the landed
contract only).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The sticky gate-status strip shows all three gate verdicts with magnitude counts simultaneously,
      each chip anchoring to its detail section. Oracle: an E2E test on a ticket passing plan review
      (blocking=0), failing completion (k/n met), and with no code review, asserting the rendered HTML
      shows all three states + counts + anchor hrefs.
- [ ] Detail sections render in the fixed order plan → completion → code regardless of which gate
      failed. Oracle: an E2E test comparing section order across two tickets with different failing
      gates.
- [ ] A finding row expands to the four-pass provenance (Finding / Verification / Decision / Coaching)
      in place, including pass-1 `evidence`, pass-2 `verification`, and pass-3 `decision` + `reason`.
      Oracle: an E2E test asserting the expanded finding HTML contains content from all four passes.
- [ ] The threshold meter renders a bar filled to `priority` with a tick at the finding's OWN resolved
      `block_threshold` (a per-finding value, not a round-level scalar), with a decision-series-derived
      fill color class. Oracle: an E2E test on a round with two findings whose persisted
      `block_threshold` values DIFFER, asserting the two tick positions differ and each fill has its
      decision-derived color class. A v1 finding (no `block_threshold`) instead renders the
      "threshold not recorded" caption.
- [ ] Findings are grouped by decision series with BLOCKING expanded and ADVISORY / DROPPED /
      INDETERMINATE collapsed-and-counted, sorted by `priority` within each group. Oracle: an E2E test
      asserting group order, expanded blocking rows, and correct collapsed counts.
- [ ] The convergence visualization renders stacked per-round bars (`<div class="conv-bars">` with one
      `.conv-bar` per round) for a ≤3-round ticket, and a line graph (`<svg class="conv-line">` with one
      `<polyline>` per non-empty decision series — NOT per round) for a ≥4-round ticket, each with an
      accompanying visually-hidden screen-reader data table. Oracle: two E2E tests — a 2-round ticket
      asserts `<div class="conv-bars">` with exactly 2 `.conv-bar` rows and NO `.conv-line` svg; a
      4-round ticket whose fixture populates K non-empty series asserts `<svg class="conv-line">` with
      exactly K `<polyline>` elements (one per series) and NO `.conv-bars`; and the visually-hidden
      `<table>` rows equal the per-round counts.
- [ ] The `◂ round n of N ▸` selector lists the retained plan- and code-review rounds newest-first,
      defaults to the latest, and selecting an OLDER round (via `?<gate>_round=<n>`) server-renders that
      round's distinct content (findings + convergence). Oracle: an E2E test seeding ≥2 rounds,
      asserting the selector shows N, defaults to latest, and requesting an older round renders its
      (distinct-from-latest) findings.
- [ ] The completion panel renders one chevron-less row per criterion from a PASS verdict's `criteria[]`
      (met glyph + kind badge + citation) and flags an `operator-attested` criterion lacking an
      attestation; for a FAIL verdict (no `criteria[]`) it renders the failure findings under an explicit
      FAIL heading. Oracle: one E2E test on a PASS completion whose `criteria[]` mixes
      `codebase-verifiable` and `operator-attested` kinds (including one operator-attested criterion with
      no recorded attestation), asserting the met glyph, kind badge, citation, and missing-attestation
      flag; and a second E2E test on a FAIL completion asserting the failure-findings fallback renders.
- [ ] A ticket with no code review and no completion verdict shows an explicit plan-review-only page
      with real `h2`/`h3` "not yet run" panels for the absent gates. Oracle: an E2E test asserting the
      empty-state panels render with headings.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
